The Calm Before (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 3
Speaker 1: Hey guys. Welcome back.
So, now we're gonna dive into the themeplates for the launch sequence. The first one,
themeplate number eight, is what I call, "The Calm Before" or The Never-too-Early
Ultimatum.
This is gonna be that pulse point number two which we spoke about earlier. There are
three of them.
That first one was that third call out email where we just really asked them, "If this isn't
something you want, feel free to check out." This is one where we're getting that buy-in
again. It's really that equivalent of a coach who right before getting into the offer says,
"Is this something you'd be interested in hearing about?" So, it's really about that gut
check. Getting that buy-in, and doing so at that crucial time, right before your offer
comes.
That's where you want to reaffirm that commitment, get that buy-in, really build that
trust and intimacy where you're frankly gonna need it most. That's what we're doing
here. We're giving them that chance to be removed and opt out once again. If they
don't remove themselves, it implies that they want to be in. That also triggers a bit of
that consistency factor. If they've decided they want to be here, knowing full well
they're gonna be exposed to an invitation and an opportunity the next day, it does
reinforce that consistency.
So, that's what we're working with here. Here is the container we're gonna be using to
deliver this content. We're gonna start off by building intimacy and connection. You can
do this any way you feel appropriate. I find that just bringing people into your reality is
intimate. Opening that window and saying, "I'm here. This is exactly what I'm doing and
feeling." That's the simplest way to build intimacy.
Just be totally transparent about where you're at. Announce that your program is going
live the next morning. So, this is an email that goes out the night before you open cart.
Just very quickly touch on the pain they'll escape from. You don't have to pour it on too
thick here. You've already done that a lot in your prelaunch.
You don't want to break away too far from that intimacy. You do want to remind them
of the pain they're gonna be escaping from as well as foreshadow and reinforce the big
outcome. Then you could put in that call to action. Ask them to reconfirm their interest.
Say, "Is this something you're interested in hearing? Is this still cool?"
Get that buy-in. Give yourself permission to continue emailing them so you can feel
invited instead of just like, "Uh, I don't really wanna send another sales email. I don't
know if they're interested." This is where you give yourself that confidence to keep
being in their inbox where you continue to feel invited to share your offer.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Then a second call to action can just remind them of that value you've already given in
the prelaunch. Then you give them that escape link. "If this isn't what you want ..." right
after you've reminded them of the value you've given them.
So, that's what's really creating that reciprocity principle. You're reminding them of
everything you've provided. That should trigger a wanting-ness for them to reciprocate,
not to escape. So you're ... I wouldn't say gaming the system, that sounds way too
manipulative. You're just being honest. I've given you this so far. If it's valuable, if it's
something you really, really want, you're in. If not here is your chance to opt out before
tomorrow. Before the offer goes live. This is how it's going to play out.
If you're wondering where this email came from, this is totally a spec piece. This is a
draft for this themeplate. I have a few others for clients, but they were just a bit too
intimate to use. As I said, this is a very intimate email. I didn't want to create a version
that I would be using. So, this is what it looks like.
10:49 PM Lower East Side. Action Needed ASAP. 10:49 Here in Lower East Side. Kind of
wish I was back home with the fam right now. Always more fun to launch these things
with loved ones nearby, but alas the cheap hotel room. Bubbly and Leonard Cohen
playlist will have to do.
This is that intimacy. By pulling back the curtain and showing them exactly where you're
at right now. This is genuine. It's sincere. It's something that they wanna see and it
should feel good to share it. To just let your audience in.
Should probably shut it down and prep for our big day, but had to reach out real quick
before the melatonin kicked in and shuttled me off to some weird smokey reverie. As
you already know, tomorrow I'll be opening the doors to the brand new "Coaching the
Conversion" certification program. When I do, you'll finally have a chance to tell those
high pressured sales tactics and persuasion tricks that up to now you, were convinced
were just the unfortunate table stakes for surviving the online marketing games. So, this
is that brief reminder of the pain and tension they're gonna get to escape and instead,
dramatically grow your revenue up to 2x in 90 days, simply by better leveraging the
things you're already great at. Coaching, educating, leading and showing your prospect
greater empathy than anyone else has previously dared.
Cheers to that.
Here's what I need you to do first.
So, here we briefly reminded them of that new outcome that's awaiting them, without
going too deep down that rabbit hole. Here's what I need you to do first, if you're still
serious about this. Notice how we use the word still and reconfirm over here, which
show and insinuate that they've already made that previous commitment. So, if you're
still serious about this, would you mind reconfirming that to me over here. It would
mean a lot, as well as give you first dibs on a special fast action bonus I'm putting

together for the first ten signups. So, give an incentive. Really incentivize this interest,
this confirmation. This is a very important buy-in. A very crucial part of your launch.
So, incentivize it however you can. If you do have a fast launch bonus, quick launch
bonus, that's gonna have limited availability or limited number of spots available for it,
this is where you could tease it. On the other hand, if you were just along for the ride,
that's an awesome value along the way. I truly feel it's time for you to step off and it
ain't just a fear talking, then here's your chance to remove yourself from the private
launch list. No harm. No foul. So, here you give them that out and it's genuine.
You're going to be selling on the next day and the next few days after that. You're gonna
be inviting them to participate. You don't want to feel tentative or timid in doing so. You
want to know that everyone who is still here right now, and you've given them a reason
to still be here right now, are people who have a great chance at still being coached into
that conversion. Either way, tomorrow is a big one for both us. So, for now, hug your
loved one for me. Sip something nice and slow. Catch you in the early AM with your
important next step. Just leading them along, guiding them, this is where you're offering
your hand as a coach and helping them with those next steps.
Then your sign off. If you want to get fancy, you by no means have to, you can use the
"yes reconfirm" link right up here to land on a special hidden page that gives your
prospect advance access to your program before everyone else. It has that sneak
preview aspect. You wouldn't advertise it in the email itself. It would just come as that
surprise, shock and leverage that sense of exclusive empowerment.
They've stumbled upon this Easter Egg throughout your marketing final. This secret late
night, backdoor link. That could be a great opportunity to give them an early access
spot. That includes something that's gonna have limited availability the next day. That
would be an advanced implementation note. If that speaks to you, if you feel that that's
something that would work for your business, your audience, by all means, go ahead
and test it. You could test it with a small segment and see how it converts. See what
kind of responses you get around it and go from there.
So, that is the email we'd be sending out the night before your launch. "The Calm
Before" or The Never-too-Early Ultimatum. Taking pulse of where your audience is at.
Getting that buy-in and just creating that intimacy right before the point where you're
gonna need it most.
So, that's it for this one. I will catch you in the next themeplate where we're going to
show you what it actually looks like to launch your product on that next day. So, I'll
catch you there in a sec.
